DISCOVERY 612, HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT QUALITY PLANNING CORPORATION'S MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE SUBPOENA WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS AS IT SEEKS IRRELEVANT DOCUMENTS WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE. SANCTIONS ARE GRANTED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,190, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Amended Mtn To Quash Or, In The Alternative, For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Apr-06-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Cynthia S. Sandoval, appeared for Defendant/Moving party
Markus Jackson, appeared for Plaintiff/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Quality Planning Corporation's Motion To Quash is granted. The court finds that the subpoena was a misuse of the discovery process as it seeks irrelevant documents without good cause. Sanctions are granted against Plaintiff in the amount of $1,190, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2006 Set For Trial On Sep-11-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Aug-23-06 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 602
